request.question: Which company focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health; Illinois Tool Works or Hologic?

title: Rain-X
Avery Berkel
ITW David Speer Academy
Shannon Free Zone
David Speer
W. James Farrell
Provena St. Mary's Hospital
Atossa Genetics
Hologic
Illinois Tool Works
sentences: Rain-X is a brand of consumer automotive and surface care products, including wiper blades owned by ITW Global Brands.
Until 2011 it was owned by Pennzoil-Quaker State Company dba SOPUS Products, a subsidiary of Royal Dutch Shell.
On March 1, 2011, Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has acquired SOPUS Products' Car Care business.
It became ITW Global, a division of Illinois Tool Works.

Avery Berkel, a major manufacturer of weighing systems and food processing equipment, was formed in 1993 when the British conglomerate General Electric Company plc combined their GEC Avery Ltd. (formerly W & T Avery Ltd.) business with the newly acquired Berkel company.
The group continued as a subsidiary of GEC (and later Marconi plc) until March 2000 when it was sold to Weigh-Tronix for £102.5 million to create Avery Weigh-Tronix.
In 2007, Avery Weigh-Tronix sold Avery Berkel to Illinois Tool Works.
ITW David Speer Academy (colloquially, Speer Academy) is a public four-year charter high school in the Belmont Cragin neighborhood in Chicago, Illinois.
It is a part of the Noble Network of Charter Schools.
It is named in memory of David B. Speer, the former Chairman of Illinois Tool Works (ITW), who served the company for 33 years until his death in 2012.
The school opened in 2014.
Shannon Free Zone is a 2.43 km2 , international business park adjacent to Shannon Airport, County Clare, Ireland which is 18 km from Ennis and 20 km from Limerick.
Businesses based on the site enjoyed special tax incentives on staff and profits until 2003.
This attracted a large number of multinational companies.
Currently there are over 100 international firms and 6,500 people employed at Shannon Free Zone in a diverse range of activities.

Companies who have invested at Shannon include Avocent, DeBeers Industrial Diamonds (Now Element Six), Kraus & Naimer, GE Capital, Precision Castparts Corp., Genworth, Ingersoll Rand, Intel, John Crane, Lufthansa Technik, Mentor Graphics, Molex, Illinois Tool Works, RSA Security, Ohshima, Schwarz Pharma and Zimmer.
David Blakeney Speer (April 6, 1951November 17, 2012) was an American businessman, best known as the CEO of Illinois Tool Works from 2005-2012.
W. James Farrell (born April 1942) is an American businessman, known for being the CEO of Illinois Tool Works from 1995 to 2005.
Provena St. Mary's Hospital is a non-profit hospital in Kankakee, Illinois.
It is a 182-bed acute care facility with a Level II Emergency/Trauma Center.

The hospital provides many health care services such as a Regional Cancer Center, Rehabilitation Services, Family Birthing Center, Pediatric Unit, Mental Health Services, Sleep Disorder Center, Breast Health Center, Renal Dialysis Centre, Accredited Chest Pain Center, Wound Care, Infusion Center, Breast Health Center, Occupational Health, Spirit of Women Program, and a Total Wellness Center.
It is part of the Presence Health healthcare system.
Provena St. Mary's is accredited by the Joint Commission and the Society of Chest Pain Centers (SCPC), as well as the American College of Surgeons Commission on Cancer.
Atossa Genetics NASDAQ: ATOS is a Seattle-based healthcare firm that specializes in breast health.
The company focuses on the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals for the treatment of breast conditions, including breast cancer.
Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.
The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.

Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.

While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".
Illinois Tool Works Inc. or ITW is a Fortune 500 company that produces engineered fasteners and components, equipment and consumable systems, and specialty products.
It was founded in 1912 by Byron L. Smith.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: Which company focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health; Illinois Tool Works or Hologic?
hop1_docs: Hologic | Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.  The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.  Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.  While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".

International Journal of Women's Health | The International Journal of Women's Health is a peer-reviewed healthcare journal focusing on all aspects of women's health care, including obstetrics, gynecology, and breast cancer.  The editor-in-chief is Elie D. Al-Chaer (University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences).  The journal is published by Dove Medical Press and it is abstracted and indexed in EMBASE and Scopus.

Women's medicine in antiquity | Childbirth and obstetrics in Classical Antiquity (here meaning the ancient Greco-Roman world) were studied by the physicians of ancient Greece and Rome.  Their ideas and practices during this time endured in Western medicine for centuries and many themes are seen in modern women's health.  Gynecology and obstetrics were originally studied and taught mainly by midwives in the ancient world, but eventually scholarly physicians of both sexes became involved as well.  Obstetrics is traditionally defined as the surgical specialty dealing with the care of a woman and her offspring during pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium (recovery).  Gynecology involves the medical practices dealing with the health of women's reproductive organs (vagina, uterus, ovaries) and their breasts.

Patient Health Questionnaire | The Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ) is a multiple-choice self-report inventory copyrighted by Pfizer Inc, that is used as a screening and diagnostic tool for mental health disorders of depression, anxiety, alcohol, eating, and somatoform disorders.  It is the self-report version of the Primary Care Evaluation of Mental Disorders (PRIME-MD), a diagnostic tool developed in the mid-1990s by Pfizer Inc.  The length of the original assessment limited its feasibility; consequently, a shorter version, consisting of 11 multi-part questions - the Patient Health Questionnaire was developed and validated.

Epocrates | Epocrates, Inc. is an American company based in San Francisco, California that makes various mobile health software applications under the "epocrates" name, mostly for health care providers, providing them reference information about drugs and diseases, and diagnostic tools.

Diagnostic Health Corporation | Diagnostic Health Corporation, based in Birmingham, Alabama, is one of the nation's largest independent diagnostic imaging companies.  The company is the former diagnostic division of HealthSouth Corporation.  The company has network of 53 free standing diagnostic imaging centers located in 19 states and the District of Columbia.

Atossa Genetics | Atossa Genetics NASDAQ: ATOS is a Seattle-based healthcare firm that specializes in breast health.  The company focuses on the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals for the treatment of breast conditions, including breast cancer.
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
- Evidence found: 
  - Hologic — passage [1] states "core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health." (passage [1]).
  - Illinois Tool Works — no matching mention in the provided passages (no passage cites Illinois Tool Works).
- Ambiguities:
  - Only one candidate page/entry (Hologic) appears in the passages. The other named company, "Illinois Tool Works," is missing from the provided passages (no candidate variants or disambiguation shown).
- Bridge entity / Missing fact:
  - Bridge entity: "Hologic" (as shown in passage [1]).
  - Missing fact needed: whether "Illinois Tool Works" has the same corporate focus (diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health) — i.e., the Illinois Tool Works lede/business segments/products descriptions confirming or denying that focus.
- Retrieval direction (prioritized):
  1) Open "Illinois Tool Works" (Wikipedia) — check the lede/infobox and "Products" or "Business segments" / "Operations" sections for mentions of diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, or skeletal health. Search keywords: "Illinois Tool Works products medical diagnostics breast gynecology skeletal".
  2) Open "Hologic" (Wikipedia) — verify lede/infobox and "Business units" or "Products" sections to corroborate the quoted focus and product examples (e.g., Selenia Dimension, Discovery bone densitometers). Search keywords: "Hologic core business units diagnostics breast health".
  3) If ITW page lacks detail, fetch "Illinois Tool Works subsidiaries" or "List of Illinois Tool Works brands" and check for any medical-device or diagnostics subsidiaries (sections or pages listing subsidiaries).
Final answer deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved.
response.hop2_query: Illinois Tool Works diagnostics breast health gynecological skeletal health
hop2_docs: Hologic | Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.  The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.  Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.  While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".

Patient Health Questionnaire | The Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ) is a multiple-choice self-report inventory copyrighted by Pfizer Inc, that is used as a screening and diagnostic tool for mental health disorders of depression, anxiety, alcohol, eating, and somatoform disorders.  It is the self-report version of the Primary Care Evaluation of Mental Disorders (PRIME-MD), a diagnostic tool developed in the mid-1990s by Pfizer Inc.  The length of the original assessment limited its feasibility; consequently, a shorter version, consisting of 11 multi-part questions - the Patient Health Questionnaire was developed and validated.

International Journal of Women's Health | The International Journal of Women's Health is a peer-reviewed healthcare journal focusing on all aspects of women's health care, including obstetrics, gynecology, and breast cancer.  The editor-in-chief is Elie D. Al-Chaer (University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences).  The journal is published by Dove Medical Press and it is abstracted and indexed in EMBASE and Scopus.

Allied health professions | Allied health professions are health care professions distinct from nursing, medicine, and pharmacy.  They work in health care teams to make the health care system function by providing a range of diagnostic, technical, therapeutic and direct patient care and support services that are critical to the other health professionals they work with and the patients they serve.

Epocrates | Epocrates, Inc. is an American company based in San Francisco, California that makes various mobile health software applications under the "epocrates" name, mostly for health care providers, providing them reference information about drugs and diseases, and diagnostic tools.

Feminist health centers | Feminist health centers are independent, not-for-profit, alternative medical facilities that primarily provide gynecological health care.  Many feminist health centers were founded in the 1970s as part of the women's health movement in the United States.  These centers were founded with the purposes of challenging the medicalization of health care, providing an alternative to mainstream health facilities, and increasing access to gynecological information and services for all women, regardless of race, class, sexual orientation, or insurance coverage.

Provena St. Mary's Hospital | Provena St. Mary's Hospital is a non-profit hospital in Kankakee, Illinois.  It is a 182-bed acute care facility with a Level II Emergency/Trauma Center.  The hospital provides many health care services such as a Regional Cancer Center, Rehabilitation Services, Family Birthing Center, Pediatric Unit, Mental Health Services, Sleep Disorder Center, Breast Health Center, Renal Dialysis Centre, Accredited Chest Pain Center, Wound Care, Infusion Center, Breast Health Center, Occupational Health, Spirit of Women Program, and a Total Wellness Center.  It is part of the Presence Health healthcare system.  Provena St. Mary's is accredited by the Joint Commission and the Society of Chest Pain Centers (SCPC), as well as the American College of Surgeons Commission on Cancer.
response.summary_2: Hologic — passage [1] states "core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health." Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.
